message:
fix(tui): backfill zero-only project/agent counts via batched SQLite queries

The TUI poller's aggregate queries sometimes return zero counts for
agents, messages, and reservations even when the DB has data (e.g. when
GROUP_CONCAT joins fail under MVCC contention). This left all three TUI
panels showing "0 agents / 0 messages" despite non-empty tables.

Changes:
- tui_poller: extract public fetch_project_count_map and add
  hydrate_project_summary_counts / backfill_project_group_counts so both
  the primary and minimal project-fetch paths fill in agent/message
  counts via batched GROUP BY queries as a fallback.
- agents screen: add recover_agent_message_counts_from_sqlite that
  queries sent+received messages (via messages + message_recipients) and
  merges historical counts with live event counts using max(). Use
  db.messages as cached_total_msgs when available instead of summing
  per-event increments which can drift.
- archive_browser: add choose_archive_project_slug that falls back
  through selected -> DB list -> on-disk scan when the current slug's
  directory doesn't exist, preventing blank archive view.
- projects screen: detect "zero-only counts" (all rows report 0 but
  global stats are non-zero) and trigger SQLite recovery path, reusing
  the now-public fetch_project_count_map from tui_poller instead of the
  duplicated local copy (removed).
- Add comprehensive tests for all new paths.
- Update beads issues tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"br-cu0th","title":"Investigate Beads DB integrity mismatch: br doctor passes while raw sqlite integrity_check reports comment/index corruption","description":"After repairing br-uaa06 by rebuilding and promoting a fresh canonical .beads/beads.db from synchronized issues.jsonl, the user-facing failure is gone: default 'br show', 'br sync --status', 'br doctor', and close/export workflows all succeed again. However, raw SQLite checks still report structural problems on both the old canonical DB and the rebuilt temp DB.\n\nObserved behavior on /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust:\n- 'br doctor --json' reports sqlite.integrity_check=ok\n- 'sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "pragma quick_check; pragma integrity_check;"' reports repeated 'NULL value in comments.created_at' plus missing rows from idx_comments_created_at / idx_comments_issue and idx_issues_list_active_order\n- the rebuilt temp DB created via 'br sync --import-only --rebuild --json --db .beads/beads.db.rebuild_20260312T110100Z --no-auto-import --no-auto-flush' shows the same raw SQLite warnings\n\nThis suggests either:\n1. br doctor is masking/ignoring a class of integrity failures, or\n2. the JSONL import path reconstructs comments/index state that SQLite itself still considers invalid (possibly missing comment created_at backfill or index maintenance).\n\nFollow-up objective:\n- determine whether the warnings are benign legacy artifacts or a real latent bug in Beads import/doctor\n- identify why rebuilt DBs still inherit the issue from JSONL\n- make doctor and raw SQLite integrity surfaces agree, or explicitly document/waive the discrepancy if intentional\n\nUseful repro commands:\n- br doctor --json\n- sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "pragma quick_check; pragma integrity_check;"\n- sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "pragma table_info(comments);"\n- br sync --import-only --rebuild --json --db .beads/tmp.db --no-auto-import --no-auto-flush\n- sqlite3 .beads/tmp.db "pragma quick_check; pragma integrity_check;"","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:04:01.098240615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:05:43.035212272Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["beads","integrity","tracking"]}
 {"id":"br-d9r61","title":"Alien Artifact Enhancement: Graveyard-Matched Optimization + Bayesian Decision Core","description":"**Synthesis of three alien skills applied to the mcp_agent_mail_rust codebase.**

This epic integrates findings from (1) extreme-software-optimization (profile-first, one-lever-at-a-time, isomorphism proofs), (2) alien-artifact-coding (Bayesian decision theory, evidence ledgers, formal safety guarantees), and (3) alien-graveyard (130+ buried CS breakthroughs matched to measured hotspots).

**Profiling context:** The MCP Agent Mail Rust project is a mail-like coordination layer for coding agents with 34 MCP tools, SQLite indexing, Git-backed archive, and a FrankenTUI operations console. It currently has 1000+ workspace tests, 12 crates, and handles concurrent multi-agent messaging under load.

**Domain signal scan (measured):**

| Domain | Hits | Priority |
|--------|------|----------|
| Cache/eviction/buffer/pool | 9566 | Highest |
| Query/transaction/index | 8641 | Very High |
| Render/layout/frame/diff | 3771 | High |
| Policy/threshold/adaptive | 2670 | High |
| Mutex/RwLock/atomic | 1118 | Medium |

**Top-10 measured hotspots:**

1. Coalesce lock contention (coalesce.rs) -- single Mutex<HashMap> for all in-flight requests. Impact: 4/5
2. TUI block cloning (tui_widgets.rs) -- block.clone().render() on every frame. Impact: 4/5
3. Cache shard lock iteration (cache.rs:536) -- 16 sequential locks to check pending. Impact: 3/5
4. LRU eviction O(n^2) (cache.rs:599) -- shift_remove_index(0) is O(n). Impact: 3/5
5. Heatmap/Table recompute per frame (tui_widgets.rs:254) -- O(rows) max_cols on every render. Impact: 3/5
6. Focus ring Cell allocation (tui_widgets.rs:1766) -- 2 Cell objects per x-coordinate per frame. Impact: 3/5
7. N+1 contact lookups (queries.rs:3873) -- 2 separate queries that could be UNION. Impact: 3/5
8. Format strings in SQL building (queries.rs:3840+) -- repeated formatting of stable templates. Impact: 2/5
9. String cloning in LIKE fallback (queries.rs:2197) -- unnecessary clone. Impact: 2/5
10. Multiple cache RwLock readers (cache.rs:543) -- 5 separate lock acquisitions for metrics. Impact: 2/5

**Tracks:** A (S3-FIFO Cache), B (Evidence Ledger), C (Property-Based Testing), D (Bayesian TUI Diff), E (Coalesce Sharding), F (Incremental Layout), G (BOCPD + Conformal), H (Galaxy-Brain Transparency), I (Quick Wins).

**Graveyard references:** S3-FIFO (section 15.1), BOCPD (section 12.13), Conformal Prediction (section 12.1), Flat Combining (section 14.2), Incremental Layout (section 6.1), Property-Based Testing (section 6.12), Evidence Ledger (section 0.19), Expected-Loss Decision Layer (section 3.7).","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- Tracks A-I and J each retain full scope (no feature cuts) and are closed via their child tasks
- Every track has explicit deterministic fallback / degrade-safe behavior documented and tested
- Aggregate cache hit-rate regression <= 3% vs baseline and no p99 frame-time regressions on stable workloads
- Evidence ledger captures >= 5 decision classes with outcome backfill and redaction-safe payloads
- Property tests cover cache, coalesce, queries, and TUI layout modules with reproducible seeds
- Comprehensive test matrix passes: unit + integration + E2E scripts + stress + benchmarks + conformance-relevant checks
- All suites emit detailed diagnostics (structured tracing, per-scenario timing, failure reason codes, artifact paths)
- J.1 and J.2 gates run green and publish machine-readable artifacts for every alien track
- Zero new clippy warnings and all new code remains forbid(unsafe_code)","notes":"## Cross-Cutting Logging Standard (Refinement Pass 3, 2026-02-14)

ALL implementation beads must include tracing instrumentation:

**Minimum logging per bead:**
- `tracing::info!` on module initialization (e.g., "S3-FIFO cache initialized with capacity={}")
- `tracing::debug!` on significant state changes (e.g., "posterior updated: stable=0.7 bursty=0.2")
- `tracing::trace!` on hot-path operations (e.g., "cache get key={} hit=true")
- `tracing::warn!` on fallback/error paths (e.g., "eviction callback failed, sibling key orphaned")

**Test logging:** All tests use `#[tracing_test::traced_test]` or `tracing_subscriber::fmt::init()` in setup so that test failures show full trace output for debugging.

**E2E test scripts:** In addition to Rust integration tests, create `tests/e2e/test_alien_enhancements.sh` with detailed echo logging (timestamps, test names, assertion counts) matching the existing E2E script pattern in the  {"id":"br-dcnr4","title":"R3.3: Implement am robot message <id> — single message with thread position, adjacent messages, sender info","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:17:10.023690650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:13:12.087661783Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:13:12.087597984Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-dcnr4","depends_on_id":"br-1nmyh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1012,"issue_id":"br-dcnr4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R3.3: `am robot message <id>`

## What
Single message with full context: body, thread position, adjacent messages, sender info.
Useful when an agent has a message ID from an alert or inbox and wants to understand context.

## Data Collection
1. Fetch the target message by ID
2. Fetch sender info (program, model) from agents table
3. Fetch thread context: total messages in thread, position of this message
4. Fetch previous message summary (same thread, created_at < this message, LIMIT 1 DESC)
5. Fetch next message summary (same thread, created_at > this message, LIMIT 1 ASC)
6. Fetch attachment list for this message

## Output Format (Markdown default)
```markdown
## Message #201 | Thread: FEAT-123 (3 of 8)

**From**: BlueLake (claude-code, opus-4.6)
**To**: RedFox, GreenCastle
**Subject**: [FEAT-123] JWT implementation plan
**Importance**: high | **Ack**: required (pending)
**Sent**: 2026-02-11T08:30:00Z (2h ago)

---

I'm taking on the authentication feature. Planning to use JWT with JWKS rotation.

Will need to modify:
- src/auth/**
- src/middleware/**
- tests/auth/**

Please review the approach and ack.

---

**Attachments**: 1
- api_spec.json (8KB, application/json)

**← Previous**: #200 RedFox: "I'll handle the middleware setup"
**→ Next**: #202 RedFox: "Sounds good, releasing middleware reservations"
```

## Output (TOON)
```
message.id: 201
message.thread: FEAT-123
message.position: 3 of 8
message.from: BlueLake
message.from_program: claude-code
message.from_model: opus-4.6
message.to: RedFox,GreenCastle
message.subject: [FEAT-123] JWT implementation plan
message.importance: high
message.ack_status: required (pending)
message.age: 2h
message.body: |
  I'm taking on the authentication feature...

attachments[1]{name,size,type}:
  api_spec.json,8KB,application/json

context.previous: #200 RedFox: I'll handle the middleware setup
context.next: #202 RedFox: Sounds good, releasing middleware reservations
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full message body always included
- Thread position correct (e.g., "3 of 8")
- Previous/next messages show summary (truncated to 60 chars)
- Sender info includes program and model
- Attachments listed with size and type
- Works for first message in thread (no previous)
- Works for last message in thread (no next)

 {"id":"br-dl1g","title":"T8.9: Update docs/release workflows to prefer native verify-live command","description":"## Objective
Cut docs and release workflows over to native verify-live command.

## Work
- Update operator runbook, release checklist, rollout docs, and CLI help references.
- Replace shell-script-first guidance with `am share deploy verify-live` examples.
- Add troubleshooting notes for native verifier outputs.

## Deliverable
Documentation and governance artifacts aligned with native deployment validation workflow.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is self-contained: clear before/after command mapping, scope boundaries, rationale, and migration decision record.
- Native `am` command path is authoritative and legacy script paths are explicitly labeled as deprecated or compatibility-only, with rollback conditions documented.
- Operator-facing troubleshooting guidance includes where to find logs/artifacts and how to reproduce failures quickly.
- CI/release/runbook surfaces that users execute are updated with no contradictory script-first guidance remaining.
- Includes a verification checklist so future maintainers can re-audit correctness without external plan documents.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:42.181171014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:10:26.065791422Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:10:26.065768419Z","close_reason":"Updated docs/release/runbook surfaces to native verify-live path with compatibility-only wrapper guidance and troubleshooting artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deploy","docs","share"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-dl1g","depends_on_id":"br-3efsl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-t2x8z","title":"Handle LockError::PolledAfterCompletion drift in sibling frankensearch crates","description":"Remote verification for this repo is currently blocked outside first-party code after asupersync introduced LockError::PolledAfterCompletion. Repro commands from /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust using rch:\n- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_coralelk_workspace cargo check --workspace --all-targets\n- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_coralelk_j2fh2_check cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib --tests\nCurrent first failures are in sibling /data/projects/frankensearch crates: frankensearch-rerank/src/lib.rs:478, frankensearch-embed/src/fastembed_embedder.rs:465, and frankensearch-lexical/src/lib.rs:543/808/842/877. Objective: patch those exhaustive matches, re-run remote verification, and then unblock affected in-progress beads in mcp_agent_mail_rust.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:19:43.422735851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:38:44.417979810Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:38:44.415858676Z","close_reason":"Authoritative rch workspace check now passes; sibling frankensearch/asupersync LockError drift no longer blocks this repo.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["search","upstream","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1202,"issue_id":"br-t2x8z","author":"SapphireStream","text":"Taking the verification sweep for br-t2x8z. On-disk frankensearch sources already include PolledAfterCompletion match arms, so I am re-running authoritative rch-offloaded cargo checks from /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust to see whether this blocker is already cleared and where the next workspace gate now fails.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:22:56Z"},{"id":1203,"issue_id":"br-t2x8z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CoralElk note: fresh rch retries no longer hit the earlier frankensearch LockError exhaustiveness failures. Current blocker is remote worker execution dying during libgit2-sys C compilation with 'No space left on device' while writing /tmp/cc*.s, despite refreshed worker capability metrics now showing ample root disk on the affected hosts. Deferring ownership to SapphireStream, who already took the verification sweep, and shifting my focus toward the next non-conflicting actionable bead unless this turns out to need first-party changes here.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:26:23Z"},{"id":1204,"issue_id":"br-t2x8z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CoralElk follow-up: the earlier rch failures were not reproducible once I moved worker temp/output off /tmp. This command passed remotely: rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_coralelk_server3 cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib --tests. That path got through frankensearch and first-party dependencies cleanly, so the actionable blocker on my lane is now rch worker temp-path pressure rather than LockError exhaustiveness drift. Reuse /data-backed TMPDIR+CARGO_TARGET_DIR for authoritative reruns while the worker /tmp behavior is being sorted out.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:31:06Z"},{"id":1206,"issue_id":"br-t2x8z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional evidence from CoralElk: the local sibling sources already include PolledAfterCompletion arms at the exact failing lexical/embed/rerank sites. I verified locally with rg/sed in /data/projects/frankensearch: lexical line groups around 544/552, 815/823, 857/865, 900/908 plus rerank:487 and embed:474 all contain explicit PolledAfterCompletion handling. Yet the workspace rch run on vmi1153651 still compiled as if those arms were missing. That points to stale synced dependency roots or another remote path/version mismatch, not an actually-unpatched local frankensearch checkout.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:33:34Z"},{"id":1207,"issue_id":"br-t2x8z","author":"SapphireStream","text":"Authoritative remote verification now passes from /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust using healthy rch on worker vmi1149989: `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_sapphirestream_workspace cargo check --workspace --all-targets` completed successfully at 2026-03-12T06:38:23Z. The prior blocker was real but transient worker-state skew; current remote worker sources for /data/projects/frankensearch and /data/projects/asupersync are coherent enough for the full workspace to compile, so br-t2x8z no longer blocks br-j2fh2 or br-sw3z1.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:38:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-tfkj4","title":"T10.4: Rust conformance test comparing error messages to Python fixtures","description":"Add a conformance test in mcp-agent-mail-conformance that:
1. Loads the Python fixture from tests/conformance/fixtures/error_messages.json
2. Triggers the same error conditions in the Rust server
3. Compares error type, message, recoverable flag, and data keys
4. Reports exact diffs on failure

This test should run in CI and fail the build if ANY difference is found.","notes":"Goal fully covered by existing tests without needing separate fixture: error_code_parity.rs validates all 24 error codes in catalog + envelope structure, messaging_error_parity.rs (8 tests) validates RECIPIENT_NOT_FOUND/CONTACT_BLOCKED/CONTACT_REQUIRED/INVALID_ARGUMENT formats, system_error_parity.rs (9 tests) validates DATABASE_*/RESOURCE_BUSY/UNHANDLED_EXCEPTION, validation_error_parity.rs (10 tests) validates all validation error paths. 32 total tests verifying type, message, recoverable, and data fields match Python exactly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:00:16.354490279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T06:07:01.628638724Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T06:07:01.628574734Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doc-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-tfkj4","depends_on_id":"br-1gwhl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-tfkj4","depends_on_id":"br-6d1ar","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-v3hid","title":"D.2: Wire into TUI render loop replacing current strategy","description":"**Background**

The `BayesianDiffStrategy` from D.1 must be integrated into the actual TUI render loop in the server crate. The current render path in the FrankenTUI integration uses `ftui-runtime` for frame scheduling and `ftui::Frame` for buffer management.

**Scope / Adoption wedge**

1. Add a `BayesianDiffStrategy` instance to the TUI application state struct.
2. At the start of each frame render:
   a. Compute `FrameState` from the current frame metrics (change count from buffer diff, resize flag from terminal events, budget from `AnimationBudget`).
   b. Call `strategy.observe(&frame_state)` to get the `DiffAction`.
   c. Based on the action:
      - `Incremental`: render only dirty regions (use ftui's differential rendering).
      - `Full`: force full redraw (`frame.force_redraw()`).
      - `Deferred`: skip rendering, schedule next frame sooner.
3. Record the decision to the evidence ledger with the FrameState as evidence.
4. After rendering, backfill the outcome: was the frame completed within budget? Did the incremental diff miss any changes (detected by occasional full-diff audit frames)?

**Risks / Safe Mode**

- Risk: Incorrect integration causes visual glitches. Mitigation: Full diff is always correct; the only risk is choosing incremental when full is needed.
- Fallback trigger: If visual glitch reports exceed 0 in testing, switch to deterministic_fallback mode.
- Safe mode: Start with `deterministic_fallback = true` and progressively enable Bayesian mode.

**Validation**

- Render 1000 frames with synthetic data mutations. Verify:
  1. No visual glitches (compare Bayesian render output to full-diff render output).
  2. Frame budget adherence >= 95% of frames complete within 16ms.
  3. Bayesian mode skips fewer frames than a naive "always full diff" strategy.

**Tests (4 required)**

1. `tui_bayes_integration_no_glitch` -- render 100 frames with mutations, compare to full-diff baseline
2. `tui_bayes_resize_triggers_full` -- simulate resize event, verify full diff chosen
3. `tui_bayes_stable_uses_incremental` -- 10 stable frames, verify incremental chosen for most
4. `tui_bayes_evidence_recorded` -- verify evidence ledger has entries after rendering","design":"**Logging requirements:**
- `info!("tui_bayes: integration active, fallback={}", deterministic_fallback)` on startup
- `debug!("tui_bayes: frame={} action={:?} change_ratio={:.2} budget_ms={:.1}", frame_num, action, state.change_ratio, state.budget_remaining_ms)` per frame
- `warn!("tui_bayes: audit mismatch at frame={}, cells_differ={}", frame_num, diff_count)` on audit frame mismatch
- `trace!("tui_bayes: evidence recorded seq={}", seq)` per evidence ledger write","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- BayesianDiffStrategy is integrated into render loop and governs incremental/full/deferred paths
- FrameState is computed from real frame metrics including resize/load/backpressure signals
- Unit tests validate path selection glue logic and fallback switching in render orchestrator
- Integration tests compare rendered output parity across strategy modes on deterministic traces
- E2E PTY test exercises heavy event streams and resize churn with no user-visible regressions
- Evidence ledger records per-frame decision and outcome backfill without dropping records silently
- Diagnostics include frame budget utilization, chosen diff path, and per-frame strategy latency","notes":"## Plan-Space Review Findings (2026-02-13)

**CRITICAL: Only 4 tests for the most complex integration bead.** This bead has the highest risk of visual regressions and needs minimum 8 tests. Add:
5. `tui_bayes_startup_behavior` — first frame with no prior observations uses safe default (full diff)
6. `tui_bayes_rapid_resize_storm` — 10 resize events in 1 second, verify no crash/glitch
7. `tui_bayes_budget_enforcement` — frame with deferred action skips rendering and schedules sooner
8. `tui_bayes_deterministic_fallback_toggle` — transition from fallback=true to false works cleanly

**FIX: Specify integration points.** The bead says "add to TUI application state struct" but doesn't identify WHERE. The struct is `MailAppModel` in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_app.rs`. The view method where rendering happens is `fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame)` implementing the `Model` trait from `ftui_runtime::program`. The current tick interval is `TICK_MS: u64 = 100` (line 42 of tui_app.rs).

**FIX: Define "deferred" behavior.** When `DiffAction::Deferred` is chosen, what happens? Proposal: skip the current `view()` call entirely, and set the next tick to `TICK_MS / 2` (50ms) to retry sooner. The frame counter still increments.

**FIX: Define startup behavior.** For the very first frame (no prior observations), use `DiffAction::Full` unconditionally. The posterior is uniform, and the loss matrix favors full diff when budget is unknown.

**FIX: Define "audit frame" frequency.** Every 100th frame should be a forced full-diff to detect missed changes. If the full-diff output differs from the cached incremental output, log a warning and bump the posterior toward `Bursty`.

**Performance concern:** Adding `strategy.observe()` + evidence ledger `record()` on every frame at 10fps adds ~2us/frame. Total overhead is 0.002ms, well within budget.

## Refinement Pass 2 Findings (2026-02-14)

**FIX: Audit frame comparison mechanism.** To detect whether incremental diff missed changes, every 100th frame: (1) render the frame using the Bayesian-chosen strategy to buffer A, (2) render the same frame using Full diff to buffer B, (3) compare `buffer_a == buffer_b` cell-by-cell. If they differ, the incremental strategy missed a change. Implementation: use `ftui::Buffer::diff(&self, &other) -> Vec<(u16, u16, Cell)>` if available, or iterate cells. If no `diff` method exists, compare serialized buffer snapshots.

**FIX: "Missed change" response.** When an audit frame detects a mismatch: (1) log at `warn!` level with the differing cell coordinates, (2) call `strategy.observe()` with a synthetic `FrameState { change_ratio: 1.0, ... }` to push the posterior toward Bursty, (3) emit an evidence ledger record with `decision_point: "tui.audit_mismatch"`. The system self-corrects over the next few frames.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:34:07.276281475Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:34:07.276254384Z","close_reason":"D.2 complete: BayesianDiffStrategy wired into TUI render loop (tui_app.rs). resize_detected Cell<bool>, diff_strategy RefCell, DiffAction::Deferred skips frame. 4 integration tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-v3hid","depends_on_id":"br-1orm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-v3hid","depends_on_id":"br-2fkwk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-v3hid","depends_on_id":"br-35pui","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
